mappings.evidence_note: Title page states 'International GCSE Physics (9-1) Specification, Pearson Edexcel International GCSE in Physics (4PH1), First teaching September 2017, First examination June 2019, Issue 4'.
Qualification consists of two externally-assessed papers: Physics Paper 1 and Physics Paper 2.
Paper 1: codes 4PH1/1P and 4SD0/1P, 61.1% of total IGCSE, 2 hours, 110 marks, availability November and June, assesses core content that is not bold and does not have a 'P' reference.
Paper 2: code 4PH1/2P, 38.9% of total IGCSE, 1 hour 15 minutes, 70 marks, availability November and June, assesses all content including bold and 'P' reference.
AO1 Knowledge and understanding of physics: 38-42% of International GCSE.
AO2 Application of knowledge and understanding, analysis and evaluation of physics: 38-42%.
AO3 Experimental skills, analysis and evaluation of data and methods in physics: 19-21%.
Topic 1 Forces and motion: units, forces and motion, Newton's laws, moments, momentum, safety.
Topic 2 Electricity: units, mains electricity, current and charge, potential difference and resistance, series and parallel circuits, magnetic effect of current.
Topic 3 Waves: units, properties of waves, sound and ultrasound, reflection and refraction, total internal reflection.
Topic 4 Energy resources and energy transfers: units, energy transfers, work and power, energy resources and electricity generation.
Topic 5 Solids, liquids and gases: units, density and pressure, change of state, ideal gas molecules.
Topic 6 Magnetism and electromagnetism: units, magnetism, electromagnetism, electromagnetic induction.
Topic 7 Radioactivity and particles: units, radioactivity, fission and fusion.
Topic 8 Astrophysics: units, origins of the Universe, lifecycle of stars, red-shift, galaxies.
Specification statements in bold with a 'P' reference relate to content in International GCSE Physics only and not in International GCSE Science (Double Award).

Command word taxonomy: Add/Label, Calculate, Comment on, Complete, Deduce, Describe, Design, Determine, Discuss, Draw, Estimate, Evaluate, Explain, Give/State/Name, Give a reason, Identify, Justify, Plot, Predict, Show that, Sketch, State what is meant by, Suggest, Analyse the data/graph to explain, What/Why/Which.
Appendix 7: Physics formulae for relationships (18 relationships).
Appendix 6: Suggested practical investigations list.
Appendix 8: Electrical circuit symbols.
Appendix 4: Mathematical skills required and assessed.

draft_notes: Page numbers use printed/page numbers from the PDF footer.
Bold 'P' reference indicates Physics-only content; Paper 2 includes it, Paper 1 excludes it.
Topic sub-point numbering (e.g., 1.1, 1.2) was reconstructed from bullet-point lists; visual review recommended.
